destinations.evidence: The nodebuilder executor explicitly logs block hash, block number, and verification error, then returns BlockExecutionError on verifier failure.
The state-hook execution path has the same explicit rejection and logging behavior.

equivalence_argument: In the v2.2 architecture the block executor lives in nodebuilder; both executor paths reject verifier errors with BlockExecutionError and structured error logging before finalization.
notes: Mapped in dependency because nodebuilder owns the Ritual block executor paths.
rationale: The old code already mapped verification errors to BlockExecutionError, but the commit explicitly centralizes handling and adds top-level error logging so verification failures are not silent. The same helper is used by both executor paths. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If block verification fails during either block executor path, execution must return a BlockExecutionError and emit an error log including block number, block hash, and the verification error before rejecting the block.
suggested_falsification_tests: Install a block verifier stub that returns an error in both execute paths, execute a block, and assert the returned error contains Block verification failed while captured logs include target ritual::block_verification, block_number, block_hash, and the original error.
summary: Nodebuilder turns block-verifier failures into explicit logged BlockExecutionError rejections.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Install a verifier stub that fails in execute_one and execute_one_with_state_hook and assert BlockExecutionError plus ritual::block_verification logs include block hash, block number, and the original error.
title: Block verification failures are explicit rejections
